title: Carnival Zombie Second Edition - A Low Effort Unboxing - A Tower Defense / Survival Zombie Game
body:
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=k3yhL30Kpnw

Daniel (@dungeondive) does a "low effort unboxing" of the long-delayed **Carnival Zombie: Second Edition**, a Kickstarter arrival he'd been eagerly awaiting on the strength of the first edition's strong reputation (Tom Vasel among its fans) and its striking, bold, slightly abstract art.

The premise hooks him immediately: Venice is built atop the back of a slumbering leviathan, and as the creature stirs the dead rise and the waters flood the lagoon — a group of survivors must flee across the sinking city. It's a cooperative, tower-defense-flavoured game for one to six where heroes largely hold the centre while zombie hordes close in; days are for recovering, moving and searching, nights for the siege.

He's delighted it's a single manageable box rather than a multi-box sprawl, praises the gorgeous board, the dark-cardboard tokens and the per-scenario reference sheets (Robinson Crusoe style), and is relieved the heroes get standees so the unnecessary minis can stay in the box. He notes some translation clunk on the packaging and a reputation as a tricky game to learn. Verdict deferred — he'll set it up, play a few times, and return with thoughts.

----

Carnival Zombie's minis are, by Daniel's own account, its least interesting component — he'd rather they'd skipped them. Do superfluous minis add value for you, or would you take standees and a lower price?
